main_ingredients: spices (cumin, coriander, garam masala, turmeric, chilli)

serving.description: 1 combo set for 2 persons (2 veg seekh kebab, 1 portion tandoori gobi, 1 portion veg jaipuri, 1 saffron rice, 2 butter naan)
serving.natural_language_portion: One full combo set for two persons, comprising two vegetable seekh kebabs, one portion of tandoori cauliflower, one portion of veg jaipuri, one saffron rice, and two butter naan, totalling approximately 900 g.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (ICMR-NIN, 2017) — composite estimation from component dishes: seekh kebab (vegetable), tandoori gobi, mixed vegetable curry, saffron rice, butter naan
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single ICMR-NIN entry exists for this composite platter. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated by weighted-average composition of the five component dishes using ICMR-NIN 2017 tables: vegetable seekh kebab (~15% by weight), tandoori gobi (~15%), veg jaipuri mixed vegetable curry (~15%), saffron rice (~30%), and butter naan (~25%). Butter and ghee finishing are accounted for in fat and saturated fat. Values represent the blended edible portion per 100 g of the combined platter.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — analogous estimate using mixed Indian vegetarian meal (rice + bread + vegetable curry components)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite tandoori vegetarian platter. GI estimated at 52 using a weighted-average approach across analogous components from the University of Sydney GI Database: saffron/basmati rice (GI ~50, 30% weight), butter naan (GI ~57, 25% weight), mixed vegetable curry (GI ~40, 30% weight), tandoori gobi and veg seekh kebab (GI ~35, 15% weight). Weighted composite GI ≈ 52 (low). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (52 × 265.5) / 100 ≈ 138.1, where carbohydrate per 900 g serving = 29.5 × 9 = 265.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from spice marinades, butter naan, and cooking salt
hypertension.guidance: Each 100 g of this platter provides approximately 310 mg sodium; a full 2-pax serving (~900 g) delivers roughly 2,790 mg sodium, approaching or exceeding the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals, so portion control and requesting reduced-salt preparation are advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High total carbohydrate load from saffron rice and butter naan despite a low composite GI
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the composite GI is estimated at 52 (low), the full 2-pax serving contains approximately 266 g of carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~138, which is very high; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should share this combo as intended for two persons, limit naan portions, and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from butter naan, ghee finishing, and yogurt marinade
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The platter provides approximately 2.8 g saturated fat per 100 g (25.2 g per 900 g serving), which is a meaningful contribution toward the ≤20 g/day saturated fat target for cardiovascular risk reduction; the vegetarian protein sources (legume-based seekh kebab, cauliflower) are cardioprotective, but butter and ghee use should be moderated.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tandoori Vegetarian Mixed Grill Combo B (2 Pax) is a North Indian vegetarian sharing platter served at Bombay Curry And Kebabs restaurant in Singapore. The combo comprises two vegetable seekh kebabs (spiced minced-vegetable and chickpea-flour patties moulded onto skewers and grilled in a clay tandoor oven), one portion of tandoori gobi (cauliflower florets marinated in spiced yogurt and char-grilled in the tandoor), one portion of veg jaipuri (a Rajasthani-style mixed vegetable curry with paneer and aromatic spices), one saffron-infused basmati rice, and two butter naan (leavened flatbreads baked against the inner wall of the tandoor and finished with butter). The tandoor imparts a characteristic smoky char and slight crispness to the grilled items while keeping the interior moist. Flavour is bold and aromatic, dominated by cumin, coriander, garam masala, turmeric, and kashmiri chilli. Nutritionally, the platter is a moderate-energy, carbohydrate-dominant meal with meaningful vegetable-derived protein, dietary fibre, and micronutrients including iron, calcium, and vitamin C. The composite glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low), primarily anchored by basmati rice and vegetable components, though the total glycemic load per full serving is high due to portion size. Sodium content is elevated from marinades and bread, making it a moderate concern for hypertensive individuals. The dish is suitable for vegetarians and is typically halal-certified in Singapore North Indian restaurants. It is a popular celebratory or group-dining option representing the tandoor tradition of Mughal-influenced North Indian cuisine.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite platter. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-average composition of five component dishes using ICMR-NIN 2017 tables, and GI is estimated by analogous-food weighting from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium because the component-level sources are authoritative but the composite estimation introduces uncertainty.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g of the combined edible platter (all five components blended by weight). 2. Serving size set at 900 g for the full 2-pax combo based on typical North Indian restaurant portion weights for this category of platter. 3. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 because this is a fully vegetarian dish with no animal flesh; vitamin B12 is naturally zero in plant-based foods without fortification — confirmed zero per ICMR-NIN vegetarian dish entries. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN 2017 for these component dishes. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN 2017 for these component dishes. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are estimated from ICMR-NIN fatty acid profiles of component ingredients. 7. GI set to 52 (low) via weighted analogous-food estimate; no direct GI measurement for this composite platter exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 8. Glycemic load per serving = (52 × 265.5) / 100 ≈ 138.1, where 265.5 g carbohydrate = 29.5 g/100 g × 900 g serving. 9. Sodium per full serving ≈ 2,790 mg — clinically significant for hypertension management. 10. The dish is vegetarian but not vegan due to butter, yogurt marinade, and possible paneer in veg jaipuri. 11. Halal status assumed based on vegetarian ingredients and typical Singapore North Indian restaurant practice; individual restaurant certification should be verified.
